ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for the specific Singaporean dessert prata variant with caramel, ice cream and speculoos. The flatbread parent class is used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Flat bread) is the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for dessert prata with toppings in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific dessert prata preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 180 g, comprising one piece of pan-fried prata topped with caramel sauce, a scoop of vanilla ice cream, and crushed speculoos biscuits.

nutrient_source.title: Prata (caramel sauce, vanilla ice cream, speculoos) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this specific dessert prata variant (cr_id F05020088). All seven reported nutrients are used as-is. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 5.2 g/100g based on the fat profile of the component ingredients (ghee-fried prata, vanilla ice cream, caramel sauce) as the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for dessert prata with caramel sauce, vanilla ice cream and speculoos. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the analogous food 'white wheat flatbread' (GI ~70, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant carbohydrate base. The high refined sugar content from caramel sauce and speculoos (17.7 g/100g) and negligible dietary fibre (0.5 g/100g) support a high-GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (70) × carbohydrate per serving (45.19 g/100g × 1.8 serving = 81.3 g) / 100 = 56.9, rounded to 56.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (17.7 g/100g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.5 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 299 mg/100g is borderline elevated with no potassium offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this dessert prata variant due to the combination of high refined sugar promoting insulin-mediated sodium retention and borderline sodium content (299 mg/100g) with no meaningful potassium offset from the toppings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 17.7 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g threshold; total carbohydrate is 45.19 g/100g with only 0.5 g/100g dietary fibre, resulting in rapid postprandial glucose absorption and a high estimated GI of 70.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this dessert prata variant; the high sugar load (17.7 g/100g), high-GI refined carbohydrate base, and negligible fibre (0.5 g/100g) will cause a rapid and pronounced postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 5.2 g/100g from ghee, ice cream and caramel, combined with high refined sugar (17.7 g/100g) and possible trans fat from speculoos and caramel processing, presents a multi-pathway cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this dessert prata; the combination of saturated fat (~5.2 g/100g), high refined sugar (17.7 g/100g), and possible trans fat from processed speculoos and caramel sauce elevates LDL cholesterol and promotes endothelial inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar (17.7 g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~5.2 g/100g), and possible trans fat from speculoos and caramel components promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dessert prata, particularly during flares, as the high refined sugar and saturated fat load from caramel sauce, vanilla ice cream and speculoos biscuits promote systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prata with Caramel Sauce, Vanilla Ice Cream and Speculoos is a dessert-style variant of the classic Singaporean roti prata — a layered, pan-fried Indian flatbread made from plain flour, ghee and egg. In this preparation, the freshly cooked prata is served warm and topped with sweet caramel sauce, a scoop of vanilla ice cream, and crushed or whole speculoos biscuits (Belgian spiced shortcrust cookies). The result is a fusion dessert combining the crispy, flaky texture of the prata with the cold creaminess of ice cream, the rich sweetness of caramel, and the cinnamon-spiced crunch of speculoos. Nutritionally, this dish is energy-dense at 288 kcal/100g, with a high carbohydrate load of 45.19 g/100g and a very high sugar content of 17.7 g/100g contributed primarily by the caramel sauce, ice cream and speculoos. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0.5 g/100g, and sodium is borderline at 299 mg/100g. The estimated glycemic index is high (~70), making this dish unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 5.2 g/100g from the ghee used in frying, the ice cream, and the caramel sauce. This dish is commonly found at Indian Muslim prata stalls and mamak restaurants across Singapore and is typically consumed as a dessert or indulgent snack. It is not recommended for regular consumption by individuals with metabolic or cardiovascular conditions.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020088, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat is estimated from component ingredients and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white wheat flatbread as the reference food, which is the closest defensible approved GI source.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.2 g/100g) is estimated from the known fat profile of the component ingredients — ghee-fried prata base (~3.0 g/100g sat fat), vanilla ice cream (~1.5 g/100g sat fat contribution), and caramel sauce (~0.7 g/100g sat fat contribution) — as the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat. This estimate should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these micronutrient and fatty acid fractions are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this composite dessert dish, and no sufficiently close approved-source equivalent was available to supply these values. 3. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white wheat flatbread (University of Sydney GI Database); the actual GI of this composite dessert prata may be higher due to the concentrated sugar from caramel sauce and speculoos. A direct GI measurement is recommended. 4. Trans fat content in speculoos biscuits and caramel sauce is uncertain; consumers should check product packaging. 5. Halal status assumes the use of halal-certified ingredients at the point of preparation; consumers should verify with the specific vendor.
